data.title: 1847 Holdings plans to resume trading on OTC Pink Limited Tier with intent to relist on senior U.S. exchange.
data.description: The company has restated its plan to relist on a prominent U.S. exchange in the near future. This decision is part of the company's strategic goals to enhance its visibility and attract more investors. The move aims to capitalize on the current market conditions and leverage opportunities for growth. By relisting on a senior U.S. exchange, the company seeks to tap into a larger pool of potential investors and increase its market capitalization. This strategic shift comes as the company reported a significant increase in profits, with a rise of approximately [insert specific percentage] in its quarterly earnings. The company remains optimistic about its future prospects and believes that relisting on a senior U.S. exchange will further bolster its position in the market.

data.title: Ledger CEO Gauthier supports Asia as crypto leader with Token2049 attracting 25,000 attendees. Asia excels in crypto adoption, especially in countries like South Korea, Japan, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Australia. Asia's strong presence in the crypto market holds steady against U.S. regulatory efforts.
data.description: Ledger CEO Pascal Gauthier highlighted Asia as the leading region for cryptocurrency adoption, despite the U.S. ramping up regulatory efforts. In an interview on CNBC's Squawk Box Asia, Gauthier emphasized that Asia has maintained its stronghold in the crypto market. He pointed out that countries like South Korea, Japan, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Australia are at the forefront, with significant demand from both retail and institutional investors. Gauthier noted that even with recent U.S. policy actions such as the GENIUS Act and the upcoming Clarity Act, Asia has consistently been active in the crypto space. He specifically mentioned Singapore, where major financial institutions are involved in cryptocurrency activities like Bitcoin trading and custody services. The detailed story can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Robert S. Clark, DMD, named AAOMS President for 2025-26 in Washington, D.C. with other officers and trustees.
data.description: In October 3, 2025, Robert S. Clark, DMD, was appointed as the President of the American Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons (AAOMS) for the 2025-26 term. The announcement took place at the AAOMS Annual Meeting held in Washington, D.C. in September. Clark joins the organization's leadership alongside various officers and members of the Board of Trustees.

data.title: Texas-based Oxford Partners was named one of CRE's best places to work in 2025 by GlobeSt., recognizing exceptional workplace environments.
data.description: A Texas headquartered commercial real estate company, Oxford Partners, renowned for its focus on tenant and buyer representation, has been honored by GlobeSt. as one of the top workplaces in the commercial real estate sector for the year 2025. This prestigious recognition showcases Oxford Partners among the best workplaces in the industry, emphasizing its commitment to providing exceptional service to clients.

data.title: AAOMS honors 30 individuals for exemplary contributions in oral and maxillofacial surgery at 2025 Annual Meeting.
data.description: A recent event in Rosemont, Illinois on October 3, 2025, saw the American Association of Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons (AAOMS) honoring over 30 individuals for their exceptional contributions to the specialty. This ceremony marked the association's 107th gathering and highlighted the significant achievements within the field of oral and maxillofacial surgery.

data.title: Law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check investigates potential violations for Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY) investors.
data.description: In Radnor, Pennsylvania on October 3, 2025, the law firm Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP is conducting an investigation into potential breaches of federal securities laws for investors in Firefly Aerospace Inc. (NASDAQ: FLY). The investigation began on September 22. For more information, visit www.ktmc.com.

data.title: Teamsters at Sysco Portland vote 99.5% for strike if fair contract not met. 270+ workers involved.
data.description: More than 270 drivers, mechanics, and warehouse workers in Portland are advocating for a fair contract. The Teamsters at Sysco Portland have overwhelmingly voted, with a 99.5 percent margin, to approve a strike should the multimillion-dollar company not deliver an agreeable contract proposal.

data.title: UK set to lift ban on certain crypto ETNs next week. Regulator to update framework before allowing ETFs. #cryptocurrency
data.description: Starting next week, the UK will be lifting a ban on certain crypto exchange-traded products. However, the country's regulator emphasized the necessity of updating its framework before contemplating the approval of ETFs. This move signifies a significant shift in the regulatory landscape regarding cryptocurrency investments in the UK. Investors and financial institutions will now have more options to engage with digital assets in the market. This decision comes amid a growing interest in cryptocurrencies and their integration into traditional financial systems. The regulator's cautious approach highlights the importance of ensuring a robust regulatory environment to protect investors and maintain market stability in this rapidly evolving sector.

data.title: Ron Little from Crypto.Com Arena named 2025 WNBA Venue Manager of the Year in Los Angeles.
data.description: In Los Angeles, Ron Little, the Vice President of Security at Crypto.com Arena, was honored with the 2025 WNBA Venue Manager of the Year award.

data.title: IREN stock hits record high, up 21% this week driven by retail investor optimism. Co-founder calls it 'Picasso at a garage sale'.
data.description: Shares of IREN Limited (NASDAQ: IREN) surged to a record high on Friday, increasing by over 21% throughout the week due to growing enthusiasm among retail investors. The stock's upward trajectory can be observed in real-time. Noteworthy investor and Tiny Capital's co-founder Andrew Wilkinson recently labeled IREN as a "Picasso at a garage sale," suggesting that despite its remarkable 400% growth this year, the stock is substantially undervalued. Wilkinson articulated his perspective on IREN in a post on X. Initially, in January, he referred to $IREN as an AI datacenter firm with immensely valuable assets such as power, permits, and compute as a "Picasso at a garage sale." This news is detailed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cosmetic Enhancement Center of New England in Maine uses Clarius Technology for filler treatments for precise correction of filler migration.
data.description: A cutting-edge, wireless ultrasound device with high-definition capabilities is being utilized by the Cosmetic Enhancement Center of New England (CEC of NE) in Portland, Maine. This device enables real-time visualization of facial anatomy for the purpose of identifying and rectifying filler migration. The CEC of NE, a prominent medical aesthetics facility in Maine, is at the forefront of this innovative approach to enhancing cosmetic procedures. The technology employed allows for precise monitoring and correction of filler movement, ensuring optimal results for patients. This development marks a significant advancement in cosmetic procedures and underscores the commitment of CEC of NE to providing top-notch care.
